title: Photochemical Reactor Control Console
description: The user operates a photochemical reactor where godrays represent UV light beams driving a photolysis reaction, and interlocking gears control reagent flow rates and catalyst mixing ratios. The goal is to balance light intensity, reagent stoichiometry, and catalyst concentration across multiple coupled inputs while obeying conservation of mass, achieving a target product yield displayed on a virtual readout.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with GLTFLoader and OrbitControls from CDN. Load godrays_demo.glb centered in the scene as a UV light source chamber emitting volumetric light into a transparent reaction vessel. Load gears.glb to the left as a mechanical control panel where each gear controls a different parameter: Gear 1 controls Reagent A flow (0-10 mol/s), Gear 2 controls Reagent B flow (0-10 mol/s), Gear 3 controls catalyst concentration (0-5 mol/L). Clicking a gear selects it (highlight with emissive outline), then mouse wheel rotates it to adjust its value. Gears must visually interlock so rotating one slightly counter-rotates adjacent gears, coupling their values (increasing A slightly decreases B, simulating shared pump pressure). Display a HUD overlay showing: current flow rates, catalyst level, reaction yield percentage, and mass balance indicator. The reaction follows A + 2B -> C with catalyst efficiency scaling yield. Godrays intensity is adjustable via a slider (0-100%) and multiplies the reaction rate. Conservation of mass must hold: total input moles must equal output moles plus unreacted remainder, shown as a live bar chart. Implement full undo/redo with Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y tracking all parameter changes in a history stack. The target is to reach exactly 85% yield while keeping mass balance error below 1%. When achieved, the reaction vessel glows green and particles emit from the godrays. Camera: perspective at (0,3,8) looking at origin. Add ambient light 0.3 intensity plus directional light from above. Background dark navy. Include a reset button and step counter showing interactions used. Minimum 10 deliberate adjustments required across all four inputs to reach the target due to coupling constraints.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  reagentAFlow: number,         // flow rate of Reagent A in mol/s (0-10)
  reagentBFlow: number,         // flow rate of Reagent B in mol/s (0-10)
  catalystConcentration: number,         // catalyst concentration in mol/L (0-5)
  lightIntensity: number,         // UV light intensity percentage (0-100)
  reactionYield: number,         // current reaction yield percentage (0-100)
  totalInputMoles: number,         // cumulative moles of A and B added to system
  totalOutputMoles: number,         // cumulative moles of product C generated
  unreactedMoles: number,         // moles of reagents remaining unreacted in vessel
  selectedGearIndex: number,         // index of currently selected gear (1=A, 2=B, 3=catalyst, 0=none)
  undoStackDepth: number,         // number of states available for undo
  massBalanceError: number,         // absolute difference between input and (output + unreacted) in moles
}
